title: The Canterbury Tales. Chaucer's Tale of Meliboeus. by Geoffrey Chaucer
content:
THE PROLOGUE.

Quoth oure Hoste; "for thou makest me

title: The Canterbury Tales. The Cook's Tale. by Geoffrey Chaucer
content:
THE PROLOGUE.

THE Cook of London, while the Reeve thus spake,
For joy he laugh'd and clapp'd him on the back:
"Aha!" quoth he, "for Christes passion,

title: The Canterbury Tales. The Shipman's Tale. by Geoffrey Chaucer
content:
THE PROLOGUE

Our Host upon his stirrups stood anon,
And saide; "Good men, hearken every one,
This was a thrifty tale for the nones.
Sir Parish Priest

title: The Canterbury Tales. Chaucer's Tale of Sir Thopas. by Geoffrey Chaucer
content:
THE PROLOGUE.

title: The Canterbury Tales. The Parson's Tale. by Geoffrey Chaucer
content:
THE PROLOGUE.

By that the Manciple his tale had ended,
The sunne from the south line was descended
So lowe, that it was not to my sight

title: To My Empty Purse
content:
To you, my purse, and to none other wight,
